name: California Mincemeat
recipeIngredient: 2 large Yellow onions; peeled and coarsely chopped
1 medium Sweet green pepper; cored, seeded & coarsely chopped
2 tbsp Ruby port, sweet Madeira OR sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, heat olive oil over moderate heat. Stir-fry onions, garlic, and green pepper for about 5 minutes until vegetables are limp.
2. Add marjoram, thyme, cumin, and cinnamon; cook for 1 to 2 minutes.
3. Add beef and pork; fry meat, breaking up large chunks, until redness disappears; do not brown the meat.
4. Stir in wine; boil uncovered for 2 to 3 minutes until it evaporates.
5. Mix remaining ingredients except almonds; bring mincemeat to a simmer and adjust heat so it simmers gently.
6. Cover skillet and cook for 45 minutes until flavors are well blended.
7. Stir in almonds; cook uncovered for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring often until heated through.
8. Serve over rice or slices of sourdough or French bread; use pita pockets or taco shells. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
